zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida van Leuven於1062年出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文時，她的父親Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel42歲，母親Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe40歲。她於1084年與Baudouin II de Hainaut在比利時海瑙特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1139年4月在比利時海瑙特蒙斯享年77歲，埋葬在比利時海瑙特蒙斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida van Leuven于1062年出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特洛文时，她的父亲Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel42岁，母亲Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe40岁。她于1084年与Baudouin II de Hainaut在比利时海瑙特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1139年4月在比利时海瑙特蒙斯享年77岁，埋葬在比利时海瑙特蒙斯。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Leuven
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerd, Inge, Merwin, Wilhelm.
North German: from the ancient Germanic personal name represented by Old English Lēofwine, composed of the elements lēof ‘dear, beloved’ + wine ‘friend’.

variants.name: Levens
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Cumbria, so named from the Old English personal name Lēofa (genitive form) + næss ‘promontory’.
North German, Dutch, and Flemish: patronymic from   Leven   2.

variants.name: Levin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Moshe, Aron, Emanuel, Yakov, Hyman, Isadore, Doron, Batya.
Jewish (mainly from Lithuania and Belarus): variant of Levy , formed with the Slavic possesive suffix -in in place of the original ending.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name Levin (see 4 below). As a Jewish name it sometimes represents a pet form of western Yiddish ‘lion’ (compare Lowe 3).

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Ida van Leuven was born in 1062, in Leuven, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, her father, Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel, was 42 and her mother, Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, was 40. She married Baudouin II de Hainaut in 1084, in Hainaut, Belgium. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died in April 1139, in Mons, Hainaut, Belgium, at the age of 77, and was buried in Mons, Hainaut, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ida van Leuven est née en 1062, à Leuven, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Baudouin II de Hainaut en 1084, à Hainaut, Belgique. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en avril 1139, à Mons, Hainaut, Belgique, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Mons, Hainaut, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ida van Leuven 1062 in Leuven, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel 42 und ihre Mutter Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe 40. Sie hat 1084 in Hennegau, Belgien Baudouin II de Hainaut geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im April 1139 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Mons, Hennegau, Belgien und wurde in Mons, Hennegau, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ida van Leuven è nata il nel 1062 nel Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, sua padre, Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Baudouin II de Hainaut nel 1084, nel Hainaut, Belgio. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel aprile 1139, nel Mons, Hainaut, Belgio, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolta a Mons, Hainaut, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Ida van Leuvenが1062年にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントレウベンで生まれた時，父Hendrik II van Leuven-Brusselは42歳，母Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuweは40歳であった。1084年にベルギーヘイナトでBaudouin II de Hainautと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1139年4月にベルギーヘイナトモンスで77歳で死去し，ベルギーヘイナトモンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ida van Leuven(이)가 1062년 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 레우븐에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1084년 벨기에 하인오트에서 Baudouin II de Hainaut(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1139년 4월 벨기에 하인오트 몬스에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 하인오트 몬스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ida van Leuven nasceu em 1062, em Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Baudouin II de Hainaut em 1084, em Hainaut, Bélgica. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em abril de 1139, em Mons, Hainaut, Bélgica, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Mons, Hainaut, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida van Leuven родилась в 1062 г. в местности леувен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baudouin II de Hainaut в 1084 г. в местности ханаут, Бельгия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла апрель 1139 г. в местности монс, ханаут, Бельгия в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности монс, ханаут, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ida van Leuven nació en 1062, en Leuven, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, su papá, Hendrik II van Leuven-Brussel, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Adela van Teisterbant en de Betuwe, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Baudouin II de Hainaut en 1084, en Hainaut, Bélgica. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en abril de 1139, en Mons, Hainaut, Bélgica, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Mons, Hainaut, Bélgica.
